---
title: "Quest 15: Functions Part 3 - Returning Values"
subtitle: "Compute and Return Results"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🎁 QUEST 15 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [View Notebook](../files/lessons/15-functions-returning.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/15-functions-returning.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Returning values keeps code reusable and testable.
This makes programs easier to reason about.

## 💡 Example

```rust
fn area(width: i32, height: i32) -> i32 {
    width * height
}

fn main() {
    let room_area = area(5, 4);
    println!("Area: {}", room_area);
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Create `is_even(n)` that returns `true` or `false`.
Try it with both odd and even inputs.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn is_even(n: i32) -> bool {
    n % 2 == 0
}

fn main() {
    println!("8 even? {}", is_even(8));
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 15 Complete
You used return types to compute reusable results.
Next: [Quest 16: Closures](16-lambda-functions.qmd)
:::
